title: ulmus campestris var. chinensis
intro: Ulmus campestris var. chinensis, also known as the Chinese Elm, is a deciduous tree species native to China and Southeast Asia, characterized by its distinctive bark and broad, rounded leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and astringent properties, making it effective in treating wounds, fever, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Ulmus campestris var. chinensis include reducing stress, improving respiratory health, and supporting the immune system. The plant can be prepared as a decoction, infusion, or powder, often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties and treat various health conditions.
